channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Last of Us Online Was Canceled ’24 Hours’ Before Public Reveal
item.content:encoded:
The director of the canceled Last of Us Online title recently opened up about how he was informed about the cancellation a day before the reveal.

For those unaware, the Last of Us Online project, also known as Factions, was canceled in 2023 as Naughty Dog shifted its focus to single-player titles. Former PlayStation executive Shuhei Yoshida thought it was great when he played it, too. Now, the director of the canceled title has provided some insights into what transpired.

Last of Us Online Was Canceled After Seven Years of Development

During a new interview on the Lance E. Lee Podcast from Tokyo (via Kotaku), Last of Us Online director Vinit Agarwal shed some light on how the game was canceled. Agarwal first spoke about the boom in the gaming industry during COVID, noting that online games experienced a significant boost around 2020. Which is when Sony, like all others, “decided to put a lot of money into online gaming”.

That is how Last of Us Online was able to secure its funding, and Agarwal stated that the “game was doing really, really well internally” with the team developing it to almost “80% completion”, it was “very close to done”. However, over the next couple of years, as people started returning to their offices, spending on online gaming dropped significantly, which led studios to reduce their projects as well.

Agarwal added that eventually the studio had to make a decision, whether to go ahead with Last of Us Online, or “make the next game that Neil Druckmann was directing.” Which basically led them to pick the game, which was “kind of the bread and butter of the studio rather than this experimental game that I was working on,” he added.

The Last of Us Online director described it as a “devastating moment” and “soul-crushing” for him, as he had spent 7 years working on the title. Moreover, he revealed that even after serving as a director on the game, he learned that The Last of Us was being canceled “24 hours before it was announced to the public.”

In other news, in a recent social media post, Neil Druckmann, the creator of The Last of Us, alluded to a little more in the pipeline. Additionally, Terminal War is a new shooter that is inspired by the canceled Last of Us Online project. What are your thoughts on Last of Us Online’s director finding out the game was canceled a day ahead of the reveal? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about GTA 5 and four other games leaving Xbox Game Pass in April 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel DLC Brings Conan the Barbarian In Big Update
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve been enjoying Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel since its September 2025 launch, then there’s some good news for you: You’ll soon be able to use the fearsome and ferocious Conan the Barbarian as a playable character as part of new DLC in a huge Jotunnslayer update.

Fresh off the press is word that Conan the Barbarian is not content with dominating in the Hyborian Age; the legendary fighter is trying his luck in Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel, fighting deities in endless loops of gameplay.

Conan the Barbarian & Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel Crossover Adds Co-Op

Conan the Barbarian is the standout inclusion in the upcoming Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel update, but it also brings with it a free co-op gameplay addition as well.

Here’s an official press release to explain more:

“In Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel, Conan arrives as a force of pure battlefield dominance. His combat style is defined by momentum and aggression, centered around his unique Triumph mechanic. By defeating enemies, Conan builds Triumph stacks that allow him to shrug off incoming damage, turning him into an unstoppable juggernaut. With the Born in Battle trait granting elevated critical lethality and the Ram Dash allowing him to plow through enemy ranks, Conan is a slayer without equal.”

Conan has three sub-classes, offering three completely unique ways of experiencing him, and the DLC explores his iconic home of Stygia.

Additionally, the update adds the God of Fate, which delivers a compelling ‘high-stakes risk-and-reward blessing system.’

Then there’s the free co-op mode, to wrap up an inviting package:

“Local Co-op launches as a FREE UPDATE.

Built and refined based on your playtest feedback, co-op is designed for shared chaos without slowing down the pace of combat.

Key features:

– two-player shared runs

– alternating level-up choices to keep a fast flow

– expanded build synergy across classes and gods

– adjusted active skill slots to preserve balance

– drop-in assistance – host retains progression

It’s a different tactical experience – more coordination, more combo potential, more battlefield moments.”

Insider Gaming is pleased to confirm that the Conan the Barbarian DLC and co-op update will arrive on April 9 for applicable platforms and consoles: PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S.

Check out the announcement trailer right here.

There are a lot of people playing Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel. Are you one of them? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord, and say why you’re loving it so much.

Check out the Steam Deck 2 targeting a 2028 release, and Nexon saying The First Descendant has ‘No Staying Power’ for more trending gaming news.
